key_points: After independence in 1947, India faced challenges such as refugee rehabilitation and integration of princely states.
The Constituent Assembly drafted the Constitution, which came into effect on 26 January 1950.
India adopted a federal democratic system with powers shared between the central and state governments.
States were reorganized mainly on linguistic lines through the States Reorganisation Act of 1956.
Economic planning through Five-Year Plans aimed to promote industrial and agricultural growth.
The Green Revolution introduced modern farming techniques and increased food grain production.
Democratic institutions such as Parliament and the Election Commission ensure participation of citizens in governance.
Scientific and technological development contributed to India's progress in fields like space research and communication.
examples: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel led the integration of princely states into the Indian Union.
Dr. B. R. Ambedkar chaired the Drafting Committee of the Constitution.
The Second Five-Year Plan emphasized heavy industries and industrial development.
Punjab and Haryana experienced significant agricultural growth during the Green Revolution.
The establishment of ISRO promoted space research and satellite technology in India.
important_terms: Federalism – A system of government in which powers are divided between central and state governments.
National Integration – The process of bringing people of different cultures, languages, and regions together as one nation.
Economic Planning – A government strategy to allocate resources and guide national economic growth.
Green Revolution – The use of high-yield seeds, fertilizers, and irrigation to increase agricultural production.
Urbanization – The growth of cities due to migration and industrial development.
exam_tips: Understand the challenges faced by India after independence and how they were addressed.
Remember the importance of the Constitution and the role of the Constituent Assembly.
Learn the objectives of Five-Year Plans and their contribution to development.
Study the causes and impacts of the Green Revolution.
Explain key concepts like federalism, democracy, and national integration with examples.
